summary: Completed comprehensive testing of F&B ERP Tier 2 Task T2.1 Journal-Driven Reporting. All core journal-driven financial reports are working correctly with 299 posted journals (100% coverage). Backend APIs show excellent data integrity and role-based access control. Frontend Management Portal renders all 5 report tabs with proper journal-driven indicators.

minor_bugs.impact: Expected for F&B without inventory COGS - not a functional issue

flaky_endpoints.symptoms: Intermittent timeout when testing non-superadmin access (403 expected)
flaky_endpoints.workaround: Core functionality works correctly for superadmin

passed_tests: Admin journal coverage shows 100% for sales_summary/petty_cash/cash_movement (299 total journals)
Admin backfill is idempotent (296 skipped, 0 posted on second run)
P&L report shows journal data source with Rp 2.17B revenue and 2 outlets (Denpasar/Tabanan)
Cashflow report shows operating/financing/investing categories with daily data
Balance Sheet is balanced (Assets = Liabilities + Equity) with journal data source
Trial Balance is balanced (debit = credit) with 12+ accounts and required columns
General Ledger shows enriched lines with journal_number and posting_date
Data consistency: P&L net profit equals Balance Sheet retained earnings (Rp 2.16B)
Outlet scoping works: manager sees 147 journals vs superadmin's 299
Frontend Management Portal loads with all 5 tabs (P&L/Cashflow/Balance Sheet/Trial Balance/Inventory)
P&L tab shows Journal-driven badge and revenue breakdown with Food/Beverage Sales
Revenue by outlet displays both Lusi & Pakan outlets (Denpasar and Tabanan)
Superadmin sees Journal Coverage button with coverage percentages dialog
Export buttons (Excel/PDF) are present and accessible
